title: Plimpton 322 PASS: Row 1 (d,e)=(12,5) — (119,120,169) triple, G/C=1;24,30 in base-60
_fixture_purpose: Anchor fixture on Row 1 of Plimpton 322, the most famous row. Direction (d,e)=(12,5): both regular (12=2²×3, 5=5¹). Triple (119,120,169). G/C=169/120=1;24,30 in base-60 (exact 2-place termination). Plimpton column 1 value: (G/C)²=(169/120)²=28561/14400. This is the largest value on the tablet (the table is ordered by decreasing G²/C², i.e., decreasing angle). The tablet was written ~1800 BCE in Old Babylonian Nippur/Larsa; recovered by Edgar Banks, sold to G.A. Plimpton ~1900. Mansfield & Wildberger 2017 (Historia Mathematica 44:395-419) established that it is a table of exact sexagesimal trigonometric values for QA-type (cot,csc) pairs.

triple._note: F=d²-e²=144-25=119 (short side β), C=2de=120 (middle), G=d²+e²=169=13² (diagonal δ)

plimpton_column1._note: Row 1 has the largest G²/C² value — largest angle from vertical in the tablet's sequence

qa_connection.chromogeometry: F=Qr(12,5)=119 (red quadrance), C=Qg(12,5)=120 (green quadrance), G=Qb(12,5)=169 (blue quadrance)
qa_connection.no_zero: F=119>0, C=120>0, G=169>0 — SPVN no-zero convention satisfied (QA A1)
qa_connection.uhg_null: Triple (119,120,169) represents null point [119:120:169] in UHG — satisfies F²+C²=G²
